prerequisites: A Brevo account with access to API credentials.
A Brevo API key, supplied via the connector's required `api_key` field.
A `start_date` value marking the earliest point from which the connector should sync data.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Brevo authenticates API requests with an API key passed in the `api-key` HTTP header against the base URL https://api.brevo.com/v3. The connector collects this key in its required secret `api_key` field.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Brevo API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Brevo account and create an API key from your account settings. Brevo passes the key in the `api-key` HTTP header on requests to https://api.brevo.com/v3 (for example, `curl -X GET https://api.brevo.com/v3/account -H "api-key: YOUR_API_KEY"`).

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the API key in the required `api_key` field and set the required `start_date` field to the earliest date from which records should be synced.

setupSteps.title: Be aware of API rate limits
setupSteps.body: Brevo enforces per-endpoint rate limits and returns a 429 Too Many Requests status when they are exceeded. Default limits vary by account tier; for example contacts endpoints allow 36,000 requests per hour (10 RPS) on standard accounts, with higher limits on Professional and Enterprise plans.
